Citations are the number of other articles citing this article, calculated by Crossref and updated daily.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Fax: +98 87336624008.Department of Chemistry, University of Kurdistan, Sanandaj 66177-15175, IranResearch Center for Nanotechnology, University of Kurdistan, Sanandaj 66177-15175, IranMore by Abdollah Salimi Article Views are the COUNTER-compliant su", "Adding Au atoms (0.2%) to the CuSn structure by galvanic replacement increases the number of active sites for nitrogen adsorption and at the same time prevents the hydrogen evolution reaction.", "The replacement of Au atoms increased the faradaic efficiency from 7.14 to 60.88%, and the NH3 yield improved from 10.94 μg h–1 cm–2 (1.78 × 10–10 mol s–1 cm–2) at −0.4 V vs RHE to 19.66 μg h–1 cm–2 (3.2 × 10–10 mol s–1 cm–2) at −0.15 V vs RHE.", "Biotechnology Advances 2026, 90 , 108898. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.biotechadv.2026.108898 Article Views are the COUNTER-compliant sum of full text article downloads since November 2008 (both PDF and HTML) across all institutions and individuals."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Fax: +98 87336624008.Department of Chemistry, University of Kurdistan, Sanandaj 66177-15175, IranResearch Center for Nan"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2949881324000283", "title": "A review of efficient electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8368099/", "title": "Efficiency and selectivity of CO2 reduction to CO on gold gas ... - For practical applications, an economical and facile method of producing catalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) is essential. Scientists at the UNIST have recently devised a new water-splitting hydrogen catalyst.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The catalyst contains ruthenium (Ru) nanoparticles uniformly distributed and anchored on the surface of multiwalled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs), or (Ru@MWCNT).", "Their results show that Ru@MWCNT produces 15.4% more hydrogen per power consumption than commercial Pt/C, and Faradaic efficiency (92.28%) is higher than Pt/C (85.97%).", "DOI: 10.1038/s41467-020-15069-3
